PAI ROBOTICS • AUTONOMOUS SYSTEMS • INVENTION

I build ambitious systems that learn, sense, and act.

Purdue Honors robotics engineer building across industrial automation, computer vision, autonomous systems, and multi-agent AI. SYSTEMS DISTRICT // SELECTED WORK

Six projects. Exact systems, ownership, constraints, and proof.

Each case study states what I built, what the team built, the technologies involved, what worked, what remains a prototype, and the engineering judgment the work demonstrates.

HONORS UX RESEARCH

INDUSTRIAL ROBOTICS

PURDUE MFET 248

FANUC Pick-and-Place + YAMAHA 5×3 Sorting Cell

Programmed two manufacturing-style robot workflows: a FANUC LR Mate 200i carrying a lock with a custom NX-designed, 3D-printed hook, and a YAMAHA YK600XGL SCARA sorting 15 chips from unknown-height stacks into a precise 5×3 grid.

  • Teach-pendant programming, position registers, sensors, I/O, buttons, lights, suction, and guarded-cell safety
  • Measured the workcell, created CAD drawings, designed end-effectors in Siemens NX, and integrated printed hardware
  • Iterated motion paths, stack-height sensing, grip/release timing, and task sequencing with a team
  • Completed both major robot projects with team scores above 94%
RESEARCH + SAFETY

MULTIMODAL AI RESEARCH

POLYGENCE + INDEPENDENT DEVELOPMENT

Improving Vehicle Safety with AI Audio-Object Detection

Developed a prototype perception pipeline that uses sound as a complementary safety signal when cameras lose confidence in fog, rain, darkness, or blind spots.

  • YOLO visual detection using nuScenes-style road data and transformer-based audio classification using AudioSet sounds
  • Synchronized audio danger flags with visual object confirmation and waveform-slope proximity categories: Far, Near, and Close
  • Research paper reports about 7% overall improvement over visual-only tests; the result is prototype evidence and still requires real-world validation
  • Sole inventor; provisional patent filed; presented at the Polygence National Conference and Purdue Shreve Tank
AUTONOMOUS ROBOTICS

AUTONOMOUS ROBOTICS

ROBOAT: AUTONOMOUS MARITIME MANEUVERS

Buoy Detection + Computer Vision Leadership

Serve as Vice President and Computer Vision Lead on a nearly 30-member autonomous boat team, connecting perception work to a physical platform.

  • Develop and review buoy-color detection workflows with Python, YOLO, Roboflow, ROS, and OAK cameras
  • Work with thousands of training images, inspect model output, identify errors, and improve dataset quality
  • Coordinate vision requirements with controls and help translate detections into actionable navigation inputs
  • Train members so model development, testing, and documentation are shared team capabilities
AI SYSTEMS

MULTI-AGENT AI

WEAVEHACKS 4

LifeOS — Six-Agent Decision Council

Built a prototype council that helps users reason across competing goals instead of treating career, finance, learning, calendar, health, and accountability as isolated lists.

  • LangGraph orchestration for recall, dispatch, conflict detection, debate, assembly, and proposed actions
  • Redis Pub/Sub, Streams, and vector memory; W&B Weave tracing; FastAPI backend; Next.js/TypeScript and CopilotKit interface
  • Developed the frontend and then took over unfinished backend work when a teammate withdrew near the deadline
  • Stabilized the system and presented the final demo solo under time pressure
SD
AI CYBERSECURITY

AI CYBERSECURITY

FRAUDFRONT / ZINNIA

Scam-Safety Discord Bot + Responsible Pilot Design

Contributed to an AI-assisted scam education and detection experience while treating privacy, abuse, rate limits, false narratives, and moderator workload as product requirements.

  • Configured and tested Discord bot runtime, slash commands, private repositories, and local API integration
  • Proposed rate limits, cooldowns, server caps, feature flags, analytics, monitoring, spam controls, and an administrative kill switch
  • Worked with PostHog planning and recruiter-facing product documentation
  • Local and pilot-stage work: presented accurately without claiming production deployment
FULL-STACK PLATFORM

FULL-STACK SOFTWARE

SCAM SPRINT / FRAUDFRONT

657-Microgame Multiplayer Platform

Built and maintained a large browser collection inspired by fast party microgames, then added online identity, rooms, teams, scores, rematches, mobile controls, and cloud state without discarding the existing games.

  • HTML, CSS, and JavaScript game systems with responsive keyboard, touch, and device interaction
  • Supabase-backed accounts, online rooms, realtime state, scores, reactions, and cross-device identity
  • Debugged race conditions, room lifecycle, rematch voting, browser persistence, score synchronization, and host/non-host behavior
  • Used AI-assisted development as a review and acceleration tool while personally integrating, testing, and maintaining the system

ROBOTICS ENGINEERING

Industrial Robotics Lab

My strongest hands-on robotics work connects mechanical design, end-effector fabrication, sensor logic, and teach-pendant programming. The result is not a generic classroom claim: it is two documented workcells with exact robot models, tasks, and constraints.

  • FANUC LR Mate 200i: custom hook end-effector and autonomous lock transfer
  • YAMAHA YK600XGL SCARA: sensor-aware sorting of 15 chips into a 5×3 grid
  • Siemens NX drawings, 3D-printed components, position registers, I/O, suction, and proximity sensing
  • Guarded-cell safety, deadman controls, iterative testing, path refinement, and team scores above 94%
Suyash DashRobotics Engineering Technology
INTERACTIVE 3D ROBOTLIVE
Drag with mouse or touch
01

FANUC LR Mate 200i

Designed and printed an NX hook attachment, mounted it to the arm, and programmed a repeatable lock-transfer sequence with teach-pendant positions and guarded-cell testing.

02

YAMAHA YK600XGL SCARA

Programmed unknown-stack-height detection, suction pickup, and precise placement of 15 chips into a 5×3 grid using sensors, registers, I/O, buttons, and lights.

03

NX + Teamcenter

Built constrained parts and top-down assemblies, including the Little Blazer Engine, while practicing design intent, revision-aware data management, and enterprise-style product workflows.

04

Reliability + Safety

Tested one motion segment at a time, protected operators with cage and deadman procedures, refined end-effector fit, and treated repeatability as a design requirement—not a final check.

HUMAN SIGNAL

Ambition matters only when it becomes dependable work.

I want recruiters to see more than potential. My record shows a pattern: I learn quickly, take ownership when plans change, translate technical work clearly, and keep iterating until a system can be demonstrated and explained.

01

Hardware + software range

I move between CAD geometry, robot cells, sensors, computer vision, AI agents, browser systems, and research documentation. That range helps me trace failures across a whole system instead of stopping at one layer.

02

Specific failure-mode thinking

My vehicle-safety research asks what happens when vision degrades. FraudFront work asks how an AI tool can be abused. Multiplayer debugging asks what happens when users, browsers, and network state disagree.

03

Ownership under pressure

At WeaveHacks, I took over unfinished backend work after a teammate withdrew, stabilized LifeOS, and presented the prototype solo. I do not wait for ideal conditions before making the next useful move.

04

Hands-on iteration

The FANUC hook, SCARA stack-height sensing, NX assemblies, model datasets, and realtime rooms all required measurement, testing, correction, and retesting—not one-shot code generation.

05

Communication that moves teams

I have presented research nationally, spoken to a graduating class, published technical explanations, trained robotics teammates, and tutored students. I make complexity understandable without pretending it is simple.

06

Evidence before adjectives

A 4.00 GPA, two Dean’s List terms, a CS 177 Python final project, documented robot projects, a provisional patent filing, and working prototypes give recruiters concrete evidence behind “resilient, innovative, and ambitious.”

07

Human-centered engineering

I am drawn to robotics because machines often enter dangerous, repetitive, or high-stakes environments. I want autonomy to extend human capability while keeping safety, clarity, and accountability visible.

MY WORKING LOOP

1Define the human and technical failure2Measure the workcell, data, or system state3Build the smallest testable path4Debug across hardware, software, and users5Document evidence, limits, and next steps
